title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?
hints: This is similar to the Fibonacci sequence.
Use memoization to optimize the recursive solution.

title: Reverse a Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
hints: Use recursion to reach the end of the list first.
On the way back, reverse the pointers.
Keep track of the new head as you unwind the recursion.

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. An integer n is a power of two if there exists an integer x such that n == 2^x.
hints: Think about the properties of powers of two in binary representation.
A power of two has exactly one bit set to 1.
Use n & (n - 1) to check if only one bit is set.

solutions.python:
def isPowerOfTwo(n: int) -> bool:
    return n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0

title: Fibonacci Number
description: The Fibonacci numbers, commonly denoted F(n), form a sequence called the Fibonacci sequence, such that each number is the sum of the two preceding ones, starting from 0 and 1. Given n, calculate F(n).
hints: Base cases: F(0) = 0, F(1) = 1.
Recursive relation: F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2).
Consider memoization to avoid redundant calculations.

title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
description: Given a string containing digits from 2-9 inclusive, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent. Return the answer in any order.
hints: Map each digit to its corresponding letters.
Use backtracking to build combinations character by character.
Process one digit at a time, appending each possible letter.
timeComplexity: O(4^n * n) where n is number of digits

title: Generate Parentheses
description: Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.
hints: Only add an open parenthesis if the count of open is less than n.
Only add a close parenthesis if the count of close is less than open.
Use backtracking to explore valid sequences.

title: Combination Sum
description: Given an array of distinct integers candidates and a target integer target, return a list of all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to target. The same number may be chosen from candidates an unlimited number of times.
hints: Sort the candidates to enable early termination.
Use backtracking with the remaining target.
Allow the same element to be reused by not incrementing the start index.
timeComplexity: O(n^(T/M)) where T is target, M is min value

title: Permutations
description: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all the possible permutations. You can return the answer in any order.
hints: Use backtracking to generate all permutations.
Swap elements to place each element at every position.
Track which elements have been used in the current permutation.

title: Pow(x, n)
description: Implement pow(x, n), which calculates x raised to the power n (i.e., x^n).
hints: Use fast exponentiation (binary exponentiation).
If n is even, x^n = (x^(n/2))^2.
If n is odd, x^n = x * x^(n-1).
Handle negative exponents by computing 1/x^(-n).

title: Subsets
description: Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set). The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the solution in any order.
hints: For each element, you can either include it or exclude it.
Use backtracking to explore both choices.
At each step, decide whether to add the current element to the subset.

title: Factorial Trailing Zeroes
description: Given an integer n, return the number of trailing zeroes in n! (n factorial). Note that n! = n * (n - 1) * (n - 2) * ... * 3 * 2 * 1.
hints: Trailing zeroes come from factors of 10, which are 2 * 5.
Count the number of times 5 appears as a factor in numbers from 1 to n.
Numbers like 25, 125 contribute multiple factors of 5.
Divide n by 5, then by 25, then by 125, etc.

title: Kth Symbol in Grammar
description: We build a table of n rows (1-indexed). We first write '0' in the 1st row. Now in every subsequent row, we look at the previous row and replace each occurrence of '0' with '01', and each occurrence of '1' with '10'. Given two integers n and k, return the kth (1-indexed) symbol in the nth row.
hints: The kth symbol in row n depends on the parent in row n-1.
If the parent is 0, the left child is 0 and right child is 1.
If the parent is 1, the left child is 1 and right child is 0.
Determine if k is in the left or right half of the row.
